title: achillea rhodoptarmica var. rosea
intro: Achillea rhodoptarmica var. rosea, also known as rose yarrow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family and is commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of essential oils, particularly borneol and bornyl acetate, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic, antispasmodic, and antiviral effects. As a herbal preparation, rose yarrow can be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to relieve headaches, fever, and respiratory issues, and is also used topically to treat wounds, skin irritations, and menstrual cramps.
